synthesis_summary: Synthesis rebuilt for a practicing-oncologist reader with four-bucket tiering (established / contested / suspected / emerging). Foundation of 32 curated landmark PMIDs (Zaretsky, Rizvi, Le, McGranahan, Tumeh, Mariathasan, Tauriello, Spranger, Peng, Skoulidis, Hugo, Siddiqui, Sade-Feldman, Alfei, Hodi, Topalian, Reck, Larkin, André, Gopalakrishnan, Routy, Sivan, Vétizou, Baruch, Davar, Miao, Gandara, Chen & Mellman, Sharma, Shin, Manguso) anchors the established tier. Additional 18 extractions from the 2026-01-20 to 2026-04-21 Europe PMC backfill populate the suspected/emerging tiers. The contested bucket explicitly flags where landmark findings have weakened: HLA LOH non-deterministic, β2M loss not uniformly resistance, microbiome taxa non-replication across geographies, Akkermansia non-monotonic, Bacteroidales sign-flip across CTLA-4 vs PD-1, TGF-β drugs clinically disappointing, bTMB failed prospectively, PBRM1 not replicated, IPRES underperformed, KEYNOTE-177 biphasic PFS + OS non-significance, WNT clinical signal weaker than mouse, IDO + pembro failed (ECHO-301), Snyder 2014 tetrapeptide signature not replicated.

top_claims.text: Pre-existing CD8+ T-cell infiltrate at the invasive tumor margin predicts pembrolizumab response in melanoma; the inflamed/excluded/desert taxonomy generalizes pan-tumor.
TMB predicts anti-PD-1 benefit in NSCLC (Rizvi 2015) and anti-CTLA-4 benefit in melanoma (Snyder 2014); pan-tumor generalization and threshold choice remain contested.
dMMR/MSI-H is the most reliable single ICI biomarker across histologies; basis of first tissue-agnostic FDA approval and of KEYNOTE-177 in 1L mCRC.
JAK1/JAK2 and B2M LOF cause acquired and primary anti-PD-1 resistance via disrupted IFN-γ sensing and MHC-I presentation; rare (<5%) but biochemically definitive.
PTPN2 loss in tumor cells sensitizes to ICI by amplifying IFN-γ/JAK-STAT response; now in clinical development.
KRAS-mutant NSCLC with STK11/LKB1 co-mutation is primary anti-PD-1 refractory despite TMB-intermediate/high status; used in NSCLC trial stratification.
PTEN loss drives ICI resistance in melanoma via PI3K-AKT activation and reduced T-cell infiltration.
TGF-β signaling in peritumoral fibroblasts drives T-cell exclusion (Mariathasan 2018 urothelial; Tauriello 2018 MSS CRC GEMM); biology established but clinical translation failed.
Response to PD-1 blockade depends on a stem-like Tcf1+PD-1+ CD8+ progenitor pool, not on reinvigoration of terminally exhausted effectors.
TOX is the master transcription factor of the exhausted CD8 chromatin state; necessary and sufficient for the exhaustion transcriptome/epigenome.
PD-1 + CTLA-4 combination improves PFS over single-agent PD-1 in advanced melanoma; largest relative benefit in PD-L1-negative tumors; durable OS at 6.5+ years.
First-line pembrolizumab monotherapy is standard for NSCLC with PD-L1 TPS ≥50%.
Ipilimumab established the durable-OS-plateau paradigm in metastatic melanoma (20–26% plateau at 10 years); nivolumab phase I extended the proof to NSCLC/RCC.
Antibiotic exposure around ICI initiation is associated with worse outcomes — the most robustly replicated microbiome observation.

HLA-I LOH is a selected immune-escape event in ~40% of NSCLCs (McGranahan 2017) but is NOT deterministic for ICI response — some HLA-LOH patients still respond depending on which allele is lost, tumor type, and residual presentation capacity.

Microbiome responder-associated taxa do not replicate across geographies. Gopalakrishnan (Faecalibacterium), Routy (Akkermansia), Matson (Bifidobacterium), Sivan/Vétizou mouse findings each identify different taxa. Meta-analyses (Lee 2022, McCulloch 2022) found no single taxon reliably predicts response.

TGF-β biology is clinically validated but TGF-β-pathway drugs (bintrafusp alfa, galunisertib, NIS793) have largely failed pivotal trials — INTR@PID lung 037 discontinued 2021. A textbook translational gap.
bTMB prospective validation failed. Gandara 2018 retrospective POPLAR/OAK signal did not confirm in prospective B-F1RST; bTMB is not broadly adopted.
PBRM1 LOF as ICI sensitizer in ccRCC did not replicate in CheckMate-009/010/025 (Braun 2020) and IMmotion analyses.
IPRES transcriptomic signature underperformed as an independent predictor; overlaps heavily with TGF-β/stromal signatures and adds little above CD8/PD-L1/TMB.
KEYNOTE-177 PFS was biphasic (~29% primary progression) and final OS did not reach statistical significance (HR ~0.74, crossover). dMMR is not a universal predictor even in 1L mCRC.
WNT/β-catenin clinical signal is weaker and more context-dependent than Spranger 2015 mouse biology predicted.
FMT from anti-PD-1 responder donors can rescue anti-PD-1-refractory melanoma (Baruch 2021 3/10; Davar 2021 6/15 with ~20% ORR). Surprising because refractoriness had been framed as tumor-intrinsic.
PD-1 on clonally expanding human T cells partially protects them from restimulation-induced cell death (in vitro). If replicated in vivo, complicates the release-the-brakes framing of anti-PD-1.
AARS1 catalyzes lactylation of PD-L1 at K280, blocking HUWE1 ubiquitination and stabilizing PD-L1; exogenous lactate paradoxically enhances anti-PD-L1 efficacy preclinically (unresolved paradox).
HILPDA-KLF5-driven lipogenesis palmitoylates PD-L1 at Cys272 and stabilizes membrane PD-L1; fenretinide engagement of TRIM21 degrades HILPDA and restores anti-PD-1 efficacy in breast cancer models.

deltaHED (germline + somatic HLA-I evolutionary divergence) predicts worse PD-1 outcomes despite higher TMB/neoantigen load across three retrospective cohorts. Inverts naïve HED prediction; needs prospective single-agent PD-1 validation.
Hypoalbuminemia causally drives ICI resistance via impaired macrophage arginine biosynthesis in LLC mice; TAM depletion or dietary arginine rescues anti-PD-1.
TROP2-claudin-7 tight junctions functionally exclude T cells from TNBC; reframes TROP2 from ADC target to barrier-mechanism target.
ATOMIC phase 3: adjuvant atezolizumab + mFOLFOX6 in stage III dMMR colon cancer, HR 0.50 for 3-year DFS (86.3% vs 76.2%); OS not yet mature.
KLRG1 nominated as novel inhibitory checkpoint in anti-PD-1-resistant melanoma; novel anti-human KLRG1 mAb reduces tumor progression in humanized KI mice via CD8/NK/γδ-T effects.
Anti-TIM-3 (TQB2618) + anti-PD-1 penpulimab achieves 52% ORR (1 CR, 10 PR) in 21 PD-1-pretreated classical Hodgkin lymphoma patients.
PKMYT1 inhibition with clinical-grade RP-6306 activates cGAS-STING and converts immune-cold CRPC to anti-PD-L1-responsive in preclinical models.
IFN-γ → IRF1 → AGPAT3 axis remodels lipids toward polyunsaturated ether phospholipids that sensitize to ferroptosis; higher AGPAT3 correlates with better ICI survival.
8-gene k-TSP + mucinous histology biomarker identifies 15% dMMR/MSI-H mCRC subgroup with 72% vs 14% 24-month PFS from anti-CTLA-4 addition to anti-PD-1 (HR 0.10). Retrospective; n=25.
LOAd703 + atezolizumab restores ICI-responsive immune signatures in 24 anti-PD-1-refractory melanoma patients; biomarker-level rescue in a hard population.
Single-cell spatial transcriptomics defines six niches stratifying neoadjuvant cSCC response better than PD-L1 IHC.
hMENA TGF-β-driven CAF 9-gene signature validated against OAK phase III in NSCLC as an ICT resistance marker.
M2 TAM-derived PGE2 via COX1/2 drives TIGIT upregulation on PD-1+ CD8+ in MSS CRC; COX2i, PGE2R antagonism, or TIGIT blockade restore anti-PD-L1 activity preclinically.

mechanism_summaries.antigen_presentation: Established: JAK1/2 and B2M LOF cause acquired and primary resistance (rare but definitive); HLA LOH is common and positively selected; dMMR predicts response pan-histology; TMB association survives across dozens of cohorts. Contested: HLA LOH is not deterministic; β2M loss does not uniformly prevent response; TMB pan-tumor threshold debated; Snyder tetrapeptide signature failed to replicate. Suspected: deltaHED inverts naïve HED prediction.
mechanism_summaries.ifn_gamma_signaling: Established: JAK1/2/STAT1 LOF drives resistance; PTPN2 loss is the tumor-intrinsic ICI sensitizer (now in clinical development); IFN-γ has dual roles (driving adaptive PD-L1 resistance while also being the effector axis anti-PD-1 exploits). Emerging: IFN-γ-IRF1-AGPAT3 extends the axis into ferroptosis sensitivity.
mechanism_summaries.tme_exclusion: Established: pre-existing CD8 infiltrate predicts response; inflamed/excluded/desert taxonomy; TGF-β signaling in CAFs excludes T cells. Contested: TGF-β drugs (bintrafusp alfa etc.) failed clinically despite compelling biology; IPRES under-replicated; WNT clinical signal weaker than mouse. Suspected: TROP2-claudin-7 barrier in TNBC; hMENA CAF signature (OAK-validated). Emerging: spatial niche stratification in cSCC; LOAd703 rescue in refractory melanoma.
mechanism_summaries.tumor_intrinsic_signaling: Established: STK11/LKB1 co-mutation drives NSCLC primary resistance; PTEN loss drives melanoma resistance. Contested: WNT/β-catenin clinical translation weaker than Spranger 2015; PBRM1 in ccRCC did not replicate. Suspected: AARS1-PD-L1 K280 lactylation (with unresolved paradox); HILPDA-KLF5-PD-L1 Cys272 palmitoylation (fenretinide rescues); NOTCH3-PVR-TIGIT in CRC. Emerging: PKMYT1-cGAS-STING activation in CRPC.
mechanism_summaries.t_cell_exhaustion: Established: Tcf1+PD-1+ stem-like CD8 progenitor pool is the operative target of PD-1 blockade, not terminally exhausted effectors; TOX is the master regulator of exhausted chromatin state (but TOX ablation also impairs persistence). Suspected: PD-1 protects expanding T cells from RICD (in vitro only); T-intrinsic VISTA synergy with anti-CTLA-4. Emerging: KLRG1 as novel checkpoint; TAM-PGE2-TIGIT in MSS CRC; anti-TIM-3 + anti-PD-1 52% ORR in PD-1-pretreated cHL.
mechanism_summaries.metabolic: Established framework: adenosine, IDO, lactate, arginine, lipid mediators all modulate ICI; IDO + pembro failed (ECHO-301). Suspected: hypoalbuminemia causally drives ICI resistance via macrophage arginine (LLC mouse); PD-L1 post-translational regulation via lipid (HILPDA) and lactate (AARS1) inputs. Theme: metabolic inputs operate on multiple substrates (killing capacity, PD-L1 stability, macrophage polarization).
mechanism_summaries.microbiome: Established: gut microbiota modulate ICI in mice; antibiotic exposure worsens outcomes (the most replicated human signal); composition differs between responders/non-responders in multiple cohorts. Contested: specific taxa (Faecalibacterium, Akkermansia, Bifidobacterium, Bacteroides) do not replicate across geographies; Akkermansia non-monotonic; Bacteroidales sign-flipped across CTLA-4 vs PD-1. Emerging (and reproducible!): FMT from responder donors rescues anti-PD-1-refractory melanoma (Baruch 2021, Davar 2021 — independent replication).
mechanism_summaries.clinical_intervention: Established: ipilimumab established the durable-OS paradigm; anti-PD-1 extended across histologies; KEYNOTE-024 set 1L NSCLC PD-L1 ≥50%; CheckMate 067 combo ipi+nivo in melanoma; KEYNOTE-177 1L dMMR mCRC. Contested: KEYNOTE-177 biphasic PFS + non-significant OS; bTMB prospective failure; pan-tumor TMB approval controversial; TGF-β and IDO combos failed. Emerging: ATOMIC adjuvant atezo in stage III dMMR (HR 0.50); 8-gene dMMR biomarker for anti-CTLA-4 addition (HR 0.10 in n=25 subgroup); LOAd703 in refractory melanoma; anti-TIM-3+PD-1 in cHL.
